It's showtime — break a leg! ✦

Be checked in by 5:00 PM and dressed & ready by 6:00 PM on show day.

Welcome, competitor

A stage built
for your craft

Thank you for stepping onto our stage for the 15th Annual Stripper Olympics. For twenty-five years Michael's has been a home for performers — and this night celebrates everything that goes into a great show: the athleticism, the artistry, the tease, and the production.

Compete with the grace and style of a consummate professional, and we'll take care of the rest. This page is your reminder of the rules from your welcome letter — keep it handy through the big night. Good luck, and have a fantastic time.

This Year's Field

The 2026 Lineup

Meet this year's competitors for the 15th Annual Stripper Olympics. Fourteen are confirmed with one final spot still open — and more headshots are arriving, so check back as the field fills in. Tap any photo to enlarge it.

14 confirmed · 1 spot still open · more photos on the way

Hosting the Night

Your Hosts

Behind the Show

Production & Stage

Jeremy also wears many hats for the show — serving as DJ, web & tech, networking, video operator, and graphic artist.

The competition

Three disciplines,
one complete performer

Every competitor is judged across three timed categories. Olympic-style scoring — seven judges per category, each line from 10 (highest) down to 1.

DIVISION 01

Lap-Dance

2:00 song

Up close, controlled, and intentional.

Attire

Tops stay on — bikini or a costume covering the private areas.

Judged on

  • Sex appeal
  • Customer focus
  • Transitions
  • Proper execution
  • Difficulty
  • Originality
  • Flexibility
DIVISION 02

Floorshow

Up to 4:00 · less is fine

Your full mini-feature production.

Judged on — Dance

  • Entertainment ability
  • Dance ability
  • Artistry of the tease
  • Stage presence

Judged on — Show

  • Costume
  • Uniqueness
  • Production

Aerial or fire is welcome — up to ⅔ of your set. Fire requires current performer fire insurance. Save heavy pole work for the Pole division.

DIVISION 03

Pole

Up to 3:00 · less is fine

Strength, control, and clean lines.

Judged on

  • Athleticism
  • Pole engagement
  • Transitions
  • Proper execution
  • Difficulty
  • Originality
  • Flexibility
  • Extensions & toe points

How it's scored

Olympic-style judging

Seven judges per category. Every line is scored from 10 down to 1, then totaled. Clean, transparent, and the same for everyone.

  • 7judges per category
  • 10highest · 1lowest
  • 3timed divisions

Background graphics and video-wall content are for the show only — they do not affect your score.

Attire & stage — by the book

  • ✓Nipples covered with pasties or latex at all times.
  • ✓Bottoms covered with a thong or booty shorts.
  • ✓Topless is optional in Floorshow & Pole only — it doesn't affect your score.
  • ✓Grip aids welcome on the poles; props welcome with placement & removal time stamps.
  • ✓Messy or wet show? Bring a pre-cut tarp sized to the stage.
  • ✕No accepting tips on stage, no uncovered nipples or bottoms, no fire without insurance.

Tipping policy

No tips during your performance. Our crew collects every tip and brings it to your dressing room the moment you step off stage.

Rehearse

Practice
schedule

Try to get most of your reps in the night before — the room is quietest then. Show-day slots fill up fast.

Sun · Jul 12

Open to everyone

The club is closed that day, so the stage is all yours — come run your whole set.

Mon · Jul 13 · Evening

Main stage open

Historically a slow night and a great time to practice with room to move.

Tue · Jul 14 · Evening

Main stage open

Same as Monday — quiet, open, and yours to rehearse on.

Wed · Jul 15 · Show day

By reserved slot only

Practice runs Noon–5:00 PM in 30-minute blocks. Reserve your time slot early with the producer.

Send your media

Music &
the video wall

Send your music — and anything you'd like on the video wall behind the stage — to Jeremy at least two weeks before the event. Tell us exactly how you want it run.

Hand over a finished video and we'll sync it to your track, or just share an idea and we'll build something for you. The video wall is pure production — it does not affect your score.

Producer · Media

Jeremy Sweigert

Email your media

Due by July 1, 2026 · two weeks out

The perks

What every competitor gets

Gift bag

A little something from us, for every contestant.

Promo packet

Professional photos and video from the night. Full delivery lands 1–2 weeks after the event.

$100 lodging credit

Toward a hotel or Air BnB for the night of the event — just bring your receipt.

Awards night

Bring a dress for the final awards ceremony — we send the night off in style.

Stay with us

The Traveling
Entertainer House

Coming in early to rehearse, or staying the weekend to work the club? Our entertainer house is open to competitors — free, any night of the week.

It's clean, secure, and women-only — no men allowed, with cameras outside. Settle in, rest up, and focus on your set.

  • 11beds
  • 4bathrooms
  • Women only
  • Cameras outside
  • Free for competitors

Want a bed? Mention it when you send your media, or reach out to the team below.

While you wait

Play DJ Swaggle

Our resident DJ has his own arcade game — a little promo for the Olympics. Take it for a spin between rehearsals.

DJ Swaggle
vs. The Algorithms ▸ Insert coin to play ◂
